recommended_when: Low-biomass air or bioaerosol metagenomics.
Taxonomic profiling, assembly, binning, and functional annotation.
required_inputs: Raw FAST5 or POD5, or already basecalled FASTQ for the target air dataset.
Configured database locations for Kraken2, Bakta, and DIAMOND-dependent downstream steps.

entry_actions.title: Review installation and database setup
entry_actions.summary: Use the air pipeline installation guide and confirm that the required databases are downloaded before running the workflow.

entry_actions.title: Configure run_pipeline paths
entry_actions.summary: Edit input, output, and database paths in the bash pipeline configuration before starting the run.

entry_actions.title: Start with the documented interactive wrapper
entry_actions.summary: Use the wrapper to launch the modular bash workflow and generate the main metagenomics outputs.

entry_actions.title: Review the generated summary outputs
entry_actions.summary: Inspect the report, read metrics, and taxonomic summaries before moving into assembly and annotation interpretation.

curated_commands.label: Launch the air metagenomics wrapper
curated_commands.notes: Recommended documented entry point for most users.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Raw FAST5 or POD5, or already basecalled FASTQ for air metagenomics.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: Use Guppy HAC for legacy FAST5 or Dorado HAC for POD5. The validated reference workflow uses Dorado HAC for POD5 entry.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: Required only when multiplexed barcoded runs have not yet been separated by sample.
preprocessing_defaults.adapter_trimming: Trim adapters with Porechop after basecalling and before NanoFilt.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 8 and minimum length 100 bp.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: Apply phase-specific read subsampling with SeqKit before taxonomic comparison when reproducing the study.

required_databases.name: Kraken2 database
required_databases.note: Primary taxonomic profiling database configured in the bash pipeline.

runtime_notes: The bash wrapper is the preferred public entry point.
Database paths must be checked manually before running the workflow.

pipeline_diagram.rationale: Starting point for all nanopore workflows.

pipeline_diagram.note: Convert raw signal to basecalled reads
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Dorado SUP gives the highest per-read accuracy for complex environmental samples.
Standard adapter removal for nanopore reads before quality filtering.
Proven environmental DNA filtering defaults (Q >= 8, min 200 bp).

pipeline_diagram.note: Classify reads against a reference database
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Established community profiling tool used in the air metagenomics study.
Flye metagenomic mode is the default assembler for air metagenomics.
Racon polishing improves consensus accuracy of Flye assemblies.
Bakta provides standardized prokaryotic genome annotation used in the air pipeline.

recommended_when: Passive wetland water studies needing branch selection.
Projects tied to the published wetland study context.
avoid_when: Water or soil matrices outside the published wetland system.
The dedicated downstream branch is already known.
required_inputs: Basecalled FASTQ directories for the relevant wetland branch.
Knowledge of which wetland track you need to reproduce.

entry_actions.title: Review the wetland study README
entry_actions.summary: Use the top-level wetland README to confirm sample mapping, accessions, and which analytical branch fits your study question.

entry_actions.title: Start from the unified wetland launcher
entry_actions.summary: Use the interactive wrapper to choose the appropriate wetland workflow and point it to the correct FASTQ directory.

curated_commands.label: Launch the wetland wrapper
curated_commands.notes: Recommended documented entry point for choosing among the three wetland branches.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Basecalled FASTQ directories for the relevant wetland branch.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: The published group workflow assumes basecalled reads at entry. The curated personal overview documents Dorado SUP for the main DNA branch.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: Required when pooled wetland reads still need per-sample separation.
preprocessing_defaults.adapter_trimming: Apply the track-specific read cleaning after selecting the correct branch.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: Track-specific thresholds apply and are summarized in the selected branch below.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: Choose the correct branch before running downstream analyses.

required_databases.name: Branch-specific taxonomic and annotation databases
required_databases.note: Kraken2, NR, or MIDORI2 may be required depending on the chosen track.

expected_outputs: Wetland-ready inputs for downstream ecological interpretation
runtime_notes: The unified wrapper only chooses the branch and handles shared preprocessing. Track-specific interpretation happens inside the selected workflow.
This playbook is the decision layer. Once the branch is clear, use the branch-specific playbook below.

pipeline_diagram.rationale: Starting point for all wetland health branches.

pipeline_diagram.note: Choose DNA metagenomics, RNA virome, or eDNA track
pipeline_diagram.rationale: The wetland health pipeline branches into three specialized tracks depending on material type.

route_summary: Environmental metagenomics -> choose the wetland branch

recommended_when: Wetland water DNA metagenomics for pathogen and AMR surveillance.
Community profiling, pathogen detection, and assembly goals.
required_inputs: Basecalled FASTQ files for wetland DNA metagenomics.
Knowledge of the correct wetland sample-to-barcode mapping.

entry_actions.title: Launch the wetland wrapper and choose the DNA branch
entry_actions.summary: Use workflow option 1 in the launcher for shotgun DNA metagenomics.

entry_actions.title: Review the DNA shotgun analysis notes
entry_actions.summary: Use the dedicated DNA shotgun analysis document to confirm the downstream metagenomics and AMR interpretation steps.

curated_commands.label: Launch the wetland wrapper
curated_commands.notes: Choose the shotgun metagenomics DNA workflow inside the interactive menu.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Basecalled FASTQ for passive-water DNA metagenomics.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: The documented group workflow assumes basecalled reads. The curated personal reference uses Dorado SUP for the wetland DNA branch.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: Demultiplex if pooled wetland DNA samples are not yet separated.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 9 and minimum length 100 bp.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: SeqKit subsampling to the published comparison depth when reproducing the benchmark branch.

required_tools.name: Assembly and profiling stack
required_tools.note: See the DNA shotgun notes for full toolchain.

required_databases.name: Metagenomics annotation databases
required_databases.note: Kraken2 and other downstream annotation resources from the central install guide.

runtime_notes: This is the primary wetland branch for pathogen and AMR surveillance.
Use the wetland sample map to confirm the correct barcode-to-site assignments before interpretation.

pipeline_diagram.note: Basecalled reads from wetland DNA samples
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Starting point for the wetland DNA metagenomics track.
Standard adapter removal before quality filtering.
Quality and length filtering for environmental DNA reads.
SeqKit provides QC metrics to guide downstream decisions.
Combined assembly and profiling for comprehensive environmental analysis.

recommended_when: RNA virome discovery from wetland water samples.
Viral community characterization rather than targeted influenza consensus.
required_inputs: Basecalled FASTQ for wetland RNA viromics.
Wetland RNA sample metadata and branch-specific output directory.

entry_actions.title: Launch the wetland wrapper and choose the RNA virome branch
entry_actions.summary: Use workflow option 4 for the wetland RNA viromics pipeline.

entry_actions.title: Review the RNA virome section
entry_actions.summary: Follow the RNA virome workflow notes in the wetland README to reproduce the viral assembly and classification branch.

curated_commands.label: Launch the wetland wrapper
curated_commands.notes: Choose the RNA virome workflow in the interactive menu.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Basecalled FASTQ for wetland RNA viromics.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: The published wetland RNA branch assumes basecalled FASTQ entry.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: Demultiplex if RNA libraries remain pooled.
preprocessing_defaults.adapter_trimming: Apply the documented RNA branch read cleaning before assembly.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 9 and minimum length 100 bp.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: Proceed into nanoMDBG assembly, Medaka polishing, and DIAMOND BLASTx classification on NR.

expected_outputs: RNA virome assemblies and polished contigs
runtime_notes: This branch is for untargeted RNA virus discovery and not for influenza-only consensus reconstruction.

pipeline_diagram.note: Basecalled reads from RNA virome material
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Starting point for the wetland RNA virome track.
Published RNA virome filtering defaults (Q >= 9, min 100 bp).

pipeline_diagram.note: Assemble viral contigs from RNA reads
pipeline_diagram.rationale: metaMDBG is optimized for short and variable-length viral reads.
Medaka polishing improves consensus for RNA-derived assemblies.
DIAMOND BLASTx provides translated viral classification from the published virome branch.

recommended_when: Wetland 12S amplicon data for vertebrate host-range inference.
Barcoded amplicon runs needing OBITools4 and MIDORI2.
avoid_when: Water eDNA outside the wetland study context.
required_inputs: Basecalled wetland 12S FASTQ data.
Wetland-specific barcode and 9 bp tag mapping.

entry_actions.title: Launch the wetland wrapper and choose the eDNA branch
entry_actions.summary: Use workflow option 2 to enter the wetland 12S metabarcoding branch.

entry_actions.title: Confirm the published barcode and tag scheme
entry_actions.summary: Use the sample map in the wetland README to verify barcode and tag assignments before demultiplexing.

curated_commands.label: Launch the wetland wrapper
curated_commands.notes: Choose the 12S metabarcoding branch inside the wrapper.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Basecalled amplicon reads for wetland 12S metabarcoding.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: The documented branch assumes basecalled amplicon reads rather than raw-signal entry.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: OBITools4 obimultiplex for barcoded 12S amplicon data.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: VSEARCH quality filtering with maxEE 1.0, singleton removal, and downstream OTU clustering.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: Global alignment against MIDORI2 followed by regional occurrence validation.

runtime_notes: Use Zambia eDNA instead if your water metabarcoding study is not tied to the wetland passive sampler system.

pipeline_diagram.rationale: Starting point for the wetland eDNA track.
OBITools4 handles amplicon-specific demultiplexing for eDNA studies.
Standard primer trimming after demultiplexing.
VSEARCH provides robust amplicon quality filtering with chimera removal.
MIDORI2 is the standard 12S reference for vertebrate metabarcoding.

route_summary: Environmental metagenomics -> amplicon workflow -> eDNA 12S -> wetland

recommended_when: Water eDNA amplicon studies using 12S or 16S metabarcoding.
Biodiversity workflows needing OBITools4 and MIDORI2.
avoid_when: Wetland-specific 12S runs where the host-range branch applies.
required_inputs: Basecalled Nanopore FASTQ or Illumina paired-end FASTQ.
The published demultiplexing reference tables from the data directory.

entry_actions.title: Review the demultiplexing reference tables
entry_actions.summary: Use the included 12S and 16S demultiplexing tables before starting OBITools-based processing.

entry_actions.title: Create the shared environment and install MIDORI2
entry_actions.summary: The documented setup uses the repository-wide environment plus a separately downloaded MIDORI2 reference database.

entry_actions.title: Choose the correct Nanopore or Illumina branch
entry_actions.summary: Start from obimultiplex for Nanopore FASTQ or obipairing plus demultiplexing for Illumina paired-end input.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Basecalled Nanopore FASTQ or Illumina paired-end FASTQ for 12S or 16S metabarcoding.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: The documented repo starts from basecalled reads. Raw-signal basecalling is outside the published workflow.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: Use obimultiplex with the published demultiplexing reference tables.
preprocessing_defaults.adapter_trimming: Cutadapt primer trimming after merging or demultiplexing.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: VSEARCH fastq filtering followed by dereplication and chimera removal.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: OTU clustering at 99% and taxonomic assignment against MIDORI2.

runtime_notes: This pipeline spans Nanopore and Illumina input modes but stays strictly at the basecalled-read stage.

pipeline_diagram.note: Basecalled reads from water eDNA amplicons
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Starting point for the Zambia eDNA workflow.
Published amplicon demultiplexing workflow for water eDNA.
Standard primer trimming after demultiplexing.
Published amplicon quality filtering and dereplication.
Published 12S taxonomic assignment workflow for water eDNA.

route_summary: Environmental metagenomics -> amplicon workflow -> eDNA 12S -> water eDNA

SLURM-oriented food safety workflows.
avoid_when: Isolate AMR profiling without adaptive-sampling comparison.

Correct local replacement of all documented placeholder paths before execution.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Basecalled BAM at entry, with conversion to FASTQ inside the workflow.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: The documented comparison often starts from already basecalled BAM files; additional basecalling is usually not the entry point here.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: Pair adaptive-sampling and native runs correctly before interpretation if samples were barcoded.
preprocessing_defaults.adapter_trimming: Adapter trimming with Porechop before downstream filtering and classification.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: Length filter >100 bp, plus project-specific downstream filtering where needed.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: Kraken2 classification and Listeria target extraction precede assembler comparison.

required_databases.name: Kraken2 database
required_databases.note: Needed for taxonomic screening and target extraction.

expected_outputs: Final HTML report comparing AS and native sequencing
runtime_notes: Full datasets are strongly documented around SLURM execution.
Most failed runs arise from unreplaced placeholder paths.

pipeline_diagram.note: Adaptive sampling output in BAM format
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Listeria adaptive sampling starts from BAM rather than raw signal.

pipeline_diagram.note: Convert BAM to FASTQ for downstream processing
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Samtools extracts reads from adaptive sampling BAM output.
Adapter removal before quality filtering.
Quality filtering for enriched reads.

pipeline_diagram.note: Screen for Listeria and background organisms
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Kraken2 identifies target and off-target reads after adaptive enrichment.
Flye assembles enriched Listeria reads into draft genomes.
AMRFinderPlus profiles AMR content in recovered Listeria assemblies.

route_summary: Food safety -> adaptive sampling or control -> Listeria

recommended_when: Rapid AMR profiling, assembly, and polishing of clinical isolates.
Single-isolate or isolate-dominant ONT datasets.
avoid_when: Complex metagenomes or multi-isolate plasmid-clustering studies.
required_inputs: Raw FAST5 or already basecalled FASTQ for the isolate.
Configured output directories for assembly and polishing.

entry_actions.title: Start with the interactive wrapper
entry_actions.summary: Use the public wrapper to collect the input data location and output directory before running the isolate workflow.

entry_actions.title: Basecall if starting from FAST5
entry_actions.summary: The README documents Guppy as the example basecaller used in this project-specific workflow description.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Raw FAST5 or already basecalled FASTQ for a bacterial isolate.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: Use the project-specific Guppy example when entering from FAST5, or start at read cleaning if basecalled reads already exist.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: Usually not needed for single-isolate runs unless barcoded inputs are still pooled.
preprocessing_defaults.adapter_trimming: Porechop before downstream filtering when reads still contain adapters.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 9 and minimum length 200 bp in the documented example.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: Proceed into Flye assembly, Minimap2 remapping, and Racon polishing.

required_databases.name: AMR interpretation resources
required_databases.note: Project-specific downstream resistance interpretation depends on the clinical analysis context.

runtime_notes: The public wrapper is the simplest way to reproduce the documented steps.

pipeline_diagram.rationale: Starting point for isolate AMR nanopore workflow.

pipeline_diagram.note: Convert raw signal to basecalled reads
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Dorado provides high-accuracy basecalling for isolate sequencing.
Standard adapter removal for nanopore reads.
Published isolate filtering defaults (Q >= 9, min 200 bp).
Flye produces high-quality isolate assemblies from long reads.
Racon polishing improves consensus accuracy for isolate assemblies.

recommended_when: CRE isolate plasmid reconstruction, typing, and clustering.
RBK114-based isolate sequencing needing per-barcode assembly.
required_inputs: Raw POD5 or basecalled FASTQ for CRE isolates.
Barcode list and an output directory for demultiplexing and assembly.

entry_actions.title: Start with the interactive wrapper
entry_actions.summary: Use the wrapper to provide your input data and output directory before running the isolate and plasmid workflow.

entry_actions.title: Basecall and demultiplex with the documented Dorado commands
entry_actions.summary: Follow the README if you want the project-specific basecalling and demultiplexing commands rather than the wrapper.

curated_commands.label: Dorado basecalling for CRE isolates
curated_commands.notes: Direct project-specific basecalling example from the README.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Raw POD5 or basecalled FASTQ for barcoded isolate data.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: Use Dorado SUP for the documented RBK114 isolate workflow when entering from POD5.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: Dorado demux for barcoded reads before downstream processing.
preprocessing_defaults.adapter_trimming: Porechop after demultiplexing when trim handling is still needed.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 8 and minimum length 200 bp in the documented example.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: Proceed into Flye assembly, Medaka polishing, MOB-Suite reconstruction, AMRFinderPlus, then Mash and clustering.

runtime_notes: This workflow is isolate-oriented and assumes barcoded per-sample separation.
Check for duplicate read IDs if multiple barcodes are merged for one sample.

pipeline_diagram.note: Raw nanopore signal files from barcoded isolates
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Starting point for the CRE plasmid clustering workflow.

pipeline_diagram.note: Convert raw signal to basecalled reads
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Dorado SUP gives the highest per-read accuracy. Used in our CRE plasmid study.
Integrated demultiplexing within Dorado for barcoded isolate pools.
Quality filtering for per-barcode isolate reads.
Flye produces complete isolate assemblies including plasmids.
Medaka polishing for high-accuracy consensus on R10.4.1 data.
MOB-Suite resolves plasmid content for epidemiological comparison.
Combined AMR profiling with Mash distance clustering and Pling plasmid network analysis.

route_summary: Clinical / isolate -> RBK114.24 or NBD114.24 -> barcoded isolate

recommended_when: Linking AMR determinants to host organisms in clinical metagenomes.
Methylation-aware association rather than standard taxonomic profiling.
avoid_when: Pure isolate AMR without host-association goals.
required_inputs: Raw POD5 files, sequencing kit name, and Kraken2 database path.
Optional reference FASTA if polishing is planned.

entry_actions.title: Start with the interactive wrapper
entry_actions.summary: Use the wrapper to collect the POD5 directory and Kraken2 database path, then orchestrate the documented standalone scripts.

entry_actions.title: Basecall, demultiplex, and filter in sequence
entry_actions.summary: Follow the standalone script order from the README if you need more control than the wrapper provides.

entry_actions.title: Run the methylation-aware host association stage
entry_actions.summary: The final Python stage links AMR genes and contigs to hosts after assembly, nanomotif, and annotation steps are complete.

curated_commands.label: Launch the AMR host association wrapper
Run the final host-association stage
curated_commands.notes: Use after the upstream assembly, motif, and annotation stages are complete.

preprocessing_defaults.input_expectation: Raw POD5 at entry, with BAM and FASTQ generated inside the workflow.
preprocessing_defaults.basecalling: Basecalling is a primary entry step, with GPU execution recommended for Dorado.
preprocessing_defaults.demultiplexing: Run the documented demux step when pooled barcodes are present.
preprocessing_defaults.adapter_trimming: Chopper-based filtering is the main public entry cleaner in this workflow.
preprocessing_defaults.length_quality_filtering: Filter reads through Chopper before assembly and BAM synchronization.
preprocessing_defaults.additional_preprocessing: Keep FASTQ and BAM in sync before assembly and downstream methylation-aware association.

required_databases.name: Kraken2 database
required_databases.note: Used strictly for annotation, not for the host-association distance logic.

runtime_notes: Kraken2 is used for annotation only, not for the host-association inference itself.

pipeline_diagram.note: Raw nanopore signal files from clinical metagenome
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Starting point for the AMR host-association workflow.

pipeline_diagram.note: Convert raw signal to basecalled reads
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Dorado SUP for maximum accuracy on complex clinical metagenomes.
Integrated demultiplexing for multiplexed clinical metagenomes.
Chopper provides flexible quality and length filtering for metagenomes.
Flye metagenomic mode for clinical metagenome assembly.
Methylation-aware analysis links modification patterns to host organisms.

pipeline_diagram.note: AMR detection, plasmid typing, and host assignment
pipeline_diagram.rationale: Combined AMR and host-association analysis for clinical metagenome interpretation.

route_summary: Clinical / isolate -> barcoded metagenome -> AMR host association
